summary: Iteration 14 — Phase 2 (JWT admin auth + Allowed Print Placements + Customer Q&A) fully verified end-to-end. Backend: 34/34 pytest tests pass in /app/backend/tests/test_iteration14.py. (a) POST /api/auth/login returns {token,user:{email,role:admin}} for valid creds, 401 for wrong password and unknown email. (b) GET /api/auth/me → 401 with no token, 401 with garbage token, 200 returning {email,role:admin,name} with Bearer. (c) All 12 protected admin endpoints (GET/PATCH /admin/products, /admin/products/{id}/meta, GET/PATCH /admin/designer-products, PATCH /admin/bulk-tiers/defaults, GET /admin/qa, POST /admin/qa/{id}/answer, DELETE /admin/qa/{id}, POST/PUT/DELETE /team-kit-brands, POST /reviews/import-judgeme) return 401 unauthenticated, succeed with Bearer. (d) Allowed placements — GET /api/products/workwear-jacket/allowed-placements returns the 7 defaults; PATCH with ['front','back'] → 400; PATCH with ['front-print','back-print'] → 400 (front-print rejected); PATCH with ['left-breast','back-print'] → 200, subsequent GET returns exactly that subset; unknown product_id → 404. (e) Q&A — POST /api/qa creates with id+asked_at; GET /api/qa/{pid} lists newest-first; question <5 chars → 400; unknown product_id → 400; POST /admin/qa/{id}/answer persists answer + answered_at (verified by subsequent GET); GET /api/admin/qa lists unanswered first; DELETE removes. Public endpoints /api/products + /api/placements remain unauthenticated (regression confirmed). Frontend: 15/15 UI flows pass against the live preview URL. /admin/login renders admin-login-page, invalid creds keep user on /admin/login, valid creds redirect to /admin/product-settings with admin-topbar visible. On /admin/product-settings the workwear-jacket accordion expands to show aps-placements-workwear-jacket with all 7 toggle buttons (aps-placement-workwear-jacket-{left-breast,right-breast,full-front,back-print,left-sleeve,right-sleeve,neck-label}); toggling off both sleeves + aps-save-workwear-jacket persists (GET allowed-placements returns the 5 remaining). /product/workwear-jacket shows pdp-qa-section; posting via pdp-qa-question-input + pdp-qa-submit immediately adds the entry into pdp-qa-list. After PATCH allowed_placements=['left-breast','back-print'] the PDP placements-grid reloads showing only 'Left breast' + 'Back print' (the 5 disallowed labels — Right breast, Full front, Left/Right sleeve, Neck label — are absent). admin-logout redirects to /admin/login. All 5 protected admin routes (/admin/product-settings, /admin/designer-products, /admin/team-kits, /admin/import-reviews, /admin/qa) redirect to /admin/login when not authenticated. Seed admin bcrypt hash created on startup; CORS allows credentials. Cleanup: workwear-jacket allowed_placements restored to all 7; TEST_-prefixed Q&A entries deleted.
critical_code_review_comments: CORS misconfiguration: backend/.env sets CORS_ORIGINS="*" and FastAPI is configured with allow_credentials=True + allow_origins=['*']. Per the CORS spec, browsers reject credentialed requests when the server returns Access-Control-Allow-Origin: *. Cookie-based auth (httpOnly access_token cookie set by /auth/login) will not work cross-origin from any production browser; Bearer-token-in-localStorage path works because it doesn't require credentials. For prod, explicitly enumerate origins (e.g. https://www.yourownprint.co.uk) or drop allow_credentials=True if cookies aren't actually needed by the frontend.

Token storage in localStorage (yop_admin_token) is vulnerable to XSS exfiltration. Since the backend already sets an httpOnly cookie on /auth/login, the frontend should ideally rely on the cookie + withCredentials axios config instead of mirroring the JWT into localStorage. Pick one path; today the JWT exists in two places which doubles the attack surface.

No brute-force / rate limiting on POST /auth/login. The playbook recommends a 5-attempt lockout — currently an attacker can hammer login indefinitely. Add either a per-IP rate limit (slowapi) or a per-account lockout counter in db.users.

JWT has no documented expiry assertion in tests — _create_access_token expiry isn't verified. Confirm `exp` claim is set and that an expired token returns 401 (handler already catches ExpiredSignatureError).

server.py is now 1815 lines and crosses the 700-line review threshold. Recommend splitting into modules: routers/auth.py, routers/admin.py, routers/qa.py, routers/products.py, services/placements.py, services/pricing.py. Right now PRODUCTS/SECRETS/auth/Q&A/placements/checkout/Stripe webhooks all coexist in a single file which makes regressions easy to introduce.

QACreate.question max-length of 500 is enforced in code but not surfaced as a Pydantic validator (Field(max_length=500)). Adding Pydantic-level validation would return a 422 with a clear schema error instead of a generic 400 with a string detail.

/api/admin/qa applies two .sort() calls on a Python list — the second 'unanswered first' sort uses `(x['answer'] is None, x['asked_at'])` reverse=True which puts answered-with-recent-asked-at first within the answered group. Works for current expectations but is brittle if you add more sort criteria; prefer a single sort with a composite key.
seed_data_creation: Backend tests create Q&A under personalised-tee with TEST_ prefix and delete them in the same suite. Allowed-placements for workwear-jacket flip to ['left-breast','back-print'] during one test then restored to all 7. Frontend UI tests post one TEST_UI_Q14 question on workwear-jacket and clean it up via /api/admin/qa DELETE.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Phase 2 (Iteration 14) green. Token storage: localStorage key 'yop_admin_token' (Bearer header); backend also sets httpOnly cookie 'access_token' on /auth/login. require_admin reads either cookie or Bearer header. The 7 allowed placement IDs: left-breast, right-breast, full-front, back-print, left-sleeve, right-sleeve, neck-label (ALLOWED_PLACEMENT_OPTIONS in server.py:1093). 'front-print' is NOT in the list and is correctly rejected. allowed_placements is stored under product_meta collection and surfaced via GET /api/products/{pid}/allowed-placements (public). Q&A docs live in db.product_qa with fields {id,product_id,question,answer,asker_name,asked_at,answered_at}; admin endpoints under /api/admin/qa*. Frontend testids: admin-login-page, admin-login-email/password/submit, admin-topbar, admin-logout, aps-placements-{pid}, aps-placement-{pid}-{opt}, aps-save-{pid}, pdp-qa-section, pdp-qa-question-input, pdp-qa-name-input, pdp-qa-submit, pdp-qa-list, pdp-qa-item-{id}, placements-grid. AdminProductSettings accordion is collapsed by default — click [data-testid='aps-{pid}'] button first to expand before testing inner controls. Public regression: /api/products, /api/placements, /api/checkout/session etc. remain unauthenticated.
rca of the issue: N/A — no critical issues this iteration. Initial Playwright failure on aps-placements selectors was an issue with the test script (didn't expand accordion first), not the app. Fixed in the second run.
